1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
3	# CTest gate. Drives tests/*.sh against pre-built artifacts in this
4	# workspace.
5	#
6	# Env overrides:
7	#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
8	#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
9	#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under verilator/build/)
10	#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
11	#   MODE         csr | pc | both  (default: both) - drives TRIGGER_MODE
12	#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
13	#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
14	#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
15	#   JOBS         parallel job slots (default: min($(nproc),
16	#                free(/dev/shm)/256MB)). Each Tier-2 slot uses a 128 MB
17	#                /dev/shm mmap and runs 3 processes (qemu1, vsim, qemu2);
18	#                the headroom check leaves margin for that plus QEMU's
19	#                own scratch. Pass JOBS=1 for sequential semantics.
20	#   LOG_DIR      where per-test logs land (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)
21	
22	set -euo pipefail
23	
24	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
25	VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/verilator"
26	HARNESS="$ROOT/tests"
27	TEST_DIR="$ROOT/tests/fixtures"
28	
29	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
30	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
31	if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
32	  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
33	fi
34	
35	step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
36	ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
37	warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
38	fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }
39	
40	require() {
41	  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
42	    fail "$1 not found: $2"
43	    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
44	    exit 1
45	  fi
46	}
47	require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
48	require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
49	require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"
50	
51	export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN
52	
53	REQUIRED_ELFS=(
54	  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
55	  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
56	  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs
57	  rt_c_v_matmul
58	  # icount_spin: CSR-less spin loop. Used by switching-mode demos
59	  # T3/T4/T5/T7 (icount, BBV emit, slice-consume, SimPoint loop).
60	  icount_spin
61	)
62	need_build=0
63	for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
64	  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
65	done
66	if (( need_build )); then
67	  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
68	  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
69	fi
70	
71	# Round-trip fixtures: name|fixture-env-prefix. All run on the unified
72	# e2e.sh harness (shared /dev/shm mmap). The TRIGGER_MODE env is
73	# injected per iteration based on $MODE. Negative drivers live in
74	# NEG_TESTS and run once regardless of mode.
75	RT_TESTS=(
76	  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3"
77	  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump"
78	  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits"
79	  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases"
80	  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi"
81	  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs"
82	  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore"
83	  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh"
84	  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival"
85	  "rt_shared_mem|FIXTURE=rt_shared_mem"
86	  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello"
87	  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf"
88	  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul TIMEOUT=120"
89	  "rt_c_v_regs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs"
90	  "rt_c_v_csrs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_csrs"
91	  "rt_c_v_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_matmul TIMEOUT=120"
92	  "rt_c_static_buf|FIXTURE=rt_c_static_buf"
93	)
94	
95	NEG_TESTS=(
96	  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/drivers/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
97	  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/drivers/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
98	  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/drivers/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
99	  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/drivers/rt_pty_handback.sh"
100	)
101	
102	# Switching-mode demos (T1-T7 in TEST_ROADMAP.md). Each demo exercises
103	# one of the M1-M8 trigger pathways end-to-end. Run once (no
104	# csr/pc/both loop) because each script hard-codes its trigger choice.
105	DEMO_TESTS=(
106	  "demo_csr_oneshot|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_csr_oneshot.sh"
107	  "demo_pc_oneshot|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_pc_oneshot.sh"
108	  "demo_icount_roundtrip|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh"
109	  "demo_bbv_profile|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_bbv_profile.sh"
110	  "demo_slice_consume_roundtrip|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh"
111	  "demo_archive_replay|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_archive_replay.sh"
112	  "demo_simpoint_loop|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_simpoint_loop.sh"
113	)
114	
115	MODE="${MODE:-both}"
116	case "$MODE" in
117	  csr|pc|both) ;;
118	  *) fail "MODE must be one of csr|pc|both (got: $MODE)"; exit 1 ;;
119	esac
120	
121	default_jobs() {
122	  local n=1 shm_free shm_cap
123	  n=$(nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 1)
124	  if [[ -d /dev/shm ]]; then
125	    shm_free=$(df -m --output=avail /dev/shm 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | tr -d ' ')
126	    if [[ "$shm_free" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$shm_free" -gt 0 ]]; then
127	      shm_cap=$((shm_free / 256))
128	      (( shm_cap < n && shm_cap >= 1 )) && n=$shm_cap
129	    fi
130	  fi
131	  (( n < 1 )) && n=1
132	  printf '%d\n' "$n"
133	}
134	JOBS="${JOBS:-$(default_jobs)}"
135	[[ "$JOBS" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$JOBS" -ge 1 ]] \
136	  || { fail "JOBS must be a positive integer (got: $JOBS)"; exit 1; }
137	
138	FILTER="${FILTER:-}"
139	
140	LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-$(mktemp -d -t e2e-XXXXXX)}"
141	mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
142	RESULTS_FILE="$LOG_DIR/results.tsv"
143	LOCK_FILE="$LOG_DIR/lock"
144	: > "$RESULTS_FILE"
145	: > "$LOCK_FILE"
146	
147	# run_one MODE NAME CMD INDEX TOTAL
148	# Captures the test command's stdout+stderr into $LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log,
149	# emits one atomic progress line, and appends a TSV row to $RESULTS_FILE
150	# (mode<TAB>name<TAB>status<TAB>rc<TAB>elapsed). flock on $LOCK_FILE
151	# serializes the progress write and the results-file append so concurrent
152	# children (introduced by the parallel-execution commit) cannot interleave
153	# their TTY writes or corrupt the tally file.
154	run_one() {
155	  local mode="$1" name="$2" cmd="$3" idx="$4" total="$5"
156	  local log="$LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log"
157	  local start end elapsed status rc
158	  start=$(date +%s)
159	  set +e
160	  bash -c "$cmd" >"$log" 2>&1
161	  rc=$?
162	  set -e
163	  end=$(date +%s)
164	  elapsed=$((end - start))
165	  case $rc in
166	    0)  status=PASS ;;
167	    77) status=SKIP ;;
168	    *)  status=FAIL ;;
169	  esac
170	  (
171	    flock 9
172	    case "$status" in
173	      PASS) ok   "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (${elapsed}s)" ;;
174	      SKIP) warn "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=77, ${elapsed}s)" ;;
175	      FAIL) fail "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=$rc, ${elapsed}s) -> $log" ;;
176	    esac
177	    printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%d\t%d\n' \
178	      "$mode" "$name" "$status" "$rc" "$elapsed" >> "$RESULTS_FILE"
179	  ) 9>"$LOCK_FILE"
180	}
181	
182	# Build the workplan: one entry per (mode, name, cmd) tuple. Order
183	# matters: csr-mode tests first, then pc-mode, then negatives (matching
184	# the original sequential layout so logs are streamed in the same order).
185	declare -a WORKPLAN=()
186	for tm in csr pc; do
187	  [[ "$MODE" != "both" && "$MODE" != "$tm" ]] && continue
188	  for entry in "${RT_TESTS[@]}"; do
189	    rt_name="${entry%%|*}"
190	    env_prefix="${entry#*|}"
191	    [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$rt_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
192	    cmd="TRIGGER_MODE=$tm $env_prefix bash $HARNESS/e2e.sh"
193	    WORKPLAN+=("$tm|$rt_name|$cmd")
194	  done
195	done
196	for entry in "${NEG_TESTS[@]}"; do
197	  neg_name="${entry%%|*}"
198	  neg_cmd="${entry#*|}"
199	  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$neg_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
200	  WORKPLAN+=("neg|$neg_name|$neg_cmd")
201	done
202	for entry in "${DEMO_TESTS[@]}"; do
203	  demo_name="${entry%%|*}"
204	  demo_cmd="${entry#*|}"
205	  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$demo_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
206	  WORKPLAN+=("demo|$demo_name|$demo_cmd")
207	done
208	
209	TOTAL=${#WORKPLAN[@]}
210	step "logs: $LOG_DIR  jobs: $JOBS  mode: $MODE  tests: $TOTAL"
211	
212	# FIFO-based bounded job pool. Each child reads one token from $SEM_FIFO
213	# before starting and writes one back on exit; only $JOBS tokens exist,
214	# so at most $JOBS children run concurrently. Opening the fifo in
215	# read-write mode (<>) means writes never block the parent. The fifo
216	# inode is unlinked immediately after fd 8 is bound -- the kernel keeps
217	# the pipe alive as long as fd 8 is open.
218	SEM_FIFO="$LOG_DIR/sem.fifo"
219	mkfifo "$SEM_FIFO"
220	exec 8<>"$SEM_FIFO"
221	rm -f "$SEM_FIFO"
222	for ((sem_i=0; sem_i<JOBS; sem_i++)); do
223	  printf '.\n' >&8
224	done
225	
226	i=0
227	for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
228	  i=$((i+1))
229	  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
230	  w_rest="${w#*|}"
231	  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
232	  w_cmd="${w_rest#*|}"
233	  read -r -u 8 _tok
234	  (
235	    run_one "$w_mode" "$w_name" "$w_cmd" "$i" "$TOTAL"
236	    printf '.\n' >&8
237	  ) &
238	done
239	wait
240	exec 8>&-
241	
242	declare -A MODE_PASS=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0 [demo]=0)
243	declare -A MODE_FAIL=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0 [demo]=0)
244	declare -A MODE_SKIP=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0 [demo]=0)
245	declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()
246	while IFS=$'\t' read -r r_mode r_name r_status _r_rc _r_el; do
247	  case "$r_status" in
248	    PASS) MODE_PASS[$r_mode]=$((MODE_PASS[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
249	    SKIP) MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]=$((MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
250	    FAIL) MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]=$((MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]+1));
251	          FAILED_NAMES+=("${r_name}[${r_mode}]") ;;
252	  esac
253	done < "$RESULTS_FILE"
254	
255	# Stream failed-test logs in workplan order so the user sees a
256	# deterministic post-run failure dump.
257	for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
258	  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
259	  w_rest="${w#*|}"
260	  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
261	  w_status=$(awk -F'\t' -v m="$w_mode" -v n="$w_name" \
262	                 '$1==m&&$2==n{print $3; exit}' "$RESULTS_FILE")
263	  if [[ "$w_status" == "FAIL" ]]; then
264	    echo
265	    echo "=== FAIL log: ${w_name}[${w_mode}] ==="
266	    cat "$LOG_DIR/$w_mode--$w_name.log"
267	  fi
268	done
269	
270	echo
271	csr_p=${MODE_PASS[csr]} csr_f=${MODE_FAIL[csr]} csr_s=${MODE_SKIP[csr]}
272	pc_p=${MODE_PASS[pc]}   pc_f=${MODE_FAIL[pc]}   pc_s=${MODE_SKIP[pc]}
273	neg_p=${MODE_PASS[neg]} neg_f=${MODE_FAIL[neg]} neg_s=${MODE_SKIP[neg]}
274	dem_p=${MODE_PASS[demo]} dem_f=${MODE_FAIL[demo]} dem_s=${MODE_SKIP[demo]}
275	total_pass=$((csr_p + pc_p + neg_p + dem_p))
276	total_fail=$((csr_f + pc_f + neg_f + dem_f))
277	total_skip=$((csr_s + pc_s + neg_s + dem_s))
278	step "summary: csr ${csr_p}p/${csr_f}f/${csr_s}s  pc ${pc_p}p/${pc_f}f/${pc_s}s  neg ${neg_p}p/${neg_f}f/${neg_s}s  demo ${dem_p}p/${dem_f}f/${dem_s}s  total ${total_pass} passed, ${total_fail} failed, ${total_skip} skipped"
279	if (( total_fail > 0 )); then
280	  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
281	  exit 1
282	fi
283

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives tests/*.sh against pre-built artifacts in this
# workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under verilator/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
#   MODE         csr | pc | both  (default: both) - drives TRIGGER_MODE
#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
#   JOBS         parallel job slots (default: min($(nproc),
#                free(/dev/shm)/256MB)). Each Tier-2 slot uses a 128 MB
#                /dev/shm mmap and runs 3 processes (qemu1, vsim, qemu2);
#                the headroom check leaves margin for that plus QEMU's
#                own scratch. Pass JOBS=1 for sequential semantics.
#   LOG_DIR      where per-test logs land (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/verilator"
HARNESS="$ROOT/tests"
TEST_DIR="$ROOT/tests/fixtures"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs
  rt_c_v_matmul
  # icount_spin: CSR-less spin loop. Used by switching-mode demos
  # T3/T4/T5/T7 (icount, BBV emit, slice-consume, SimPoint loop).
  icount_spin
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
done
if (( need_build )); then
  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
fi

# Round-trip fixtures: name|fixture-env-prefix. All run on the unified
# e2e.sh harness (shared /dev/shm mmap). The TRIGGER_MODE env is
# injected per iteration based on $MODE. Negative drivers live in
# NEG_TESTS and run once regardless of mode.
RT_TESTS=(
  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3"
  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump"
  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits"
  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases"
  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi"
  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs"
  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore"
  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh"
  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival"
  "rt_shared_mem|FIXTURE=rt_shared_mem"
  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello"
  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf"
  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul TIMEOUT=120"
  "rt_c_v_regs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs"
  "rt_c_v_csrs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_csrs"
  "rt_c_v_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_matmul TIMEOUT=120"
  "rt_c_static_buf|FIXTURE=rt_c_static_buf"
)

NEG_TESTS=(
  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/drivers/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/drivers/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/drivers/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/drivers/rt_pty_handback.sh"
)

# Switching-mode demos (T1-T7 in TEST_ROADMAP.md). Each demo exercises
# one of the M1-M8 trigger pathways end-to-end. Run once (no
# csr/pc/both loop) because each script hard-codes its trigger choice.
DEMO_TESTS=(
  "demo_csr_oneshot|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_csr_oneshot.sh"
  "demo_pc_oneshot|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_pc_oneshot.sh"
  "demo_icount_roundtrip|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh"
  "demo_bbv_profile|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_bbv_profile.sh"
  "demo_slice_consume_roundtrip|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh"
  "demo_archive_replay|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_archive_replay.sh"
  "demo_simpoint_loop|bash $HARNESS/demos/demo_simpoint_loop.sh"
)

MODE="${MODE:-both}"
case "$MODE" in
  csr|pc|both) ;;
  *) fail "MODE must be one of csr|pc|both (got: $MODE)"; exit 1 ;;
esac

default_jobs() {
  local n=1 shm_free shm_cap
  n=$(nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 1)
  if [[ -d /dev/shm ]]; then
    shm_free=$(df -m --output=avail /dev/shm 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | tr -d ' ')
    if [[ "$shm_free" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$shm_free" -gt 0 ]]; then
      shm_cap=$((shm_free / 256))
      (( shm_cap < n && shm_cap >= 1 )) && n=$shm_cap
    fi
  fi
  (( n < 1 )) && n=1
  printf '%d\n' "$n"
}
JOBS="${JOBS:-$(default_jobs)}"
[[ "$JOBS" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$JOBS" -ge 1 ]] \
  || { fail "JOBS must be a positive integer (got: $JOBS)"; exit 1; }

FILTER="${FILTER:-}"

LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-$(mktemp -d -t e2e-XXXXXX)}"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
RESULTS_FILE="$LOG_DIR/results.tsv"
LOCK_FILE="$LOG_DIR/lock"
: > "$RESULTS_FILE"
: > "$LOCK_FILE"

# run_one MODE NAME CMD INDEX TOTAL
# Captures the test command's stdout+stderr into $LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log,
# emits one atomic progress line, and appends a TSV row to $RESULTS_FILE
# (mode<TAB>name<TAB>status<TAB>rc<TAB>elapsed). flock on $LOCK_FILE
# serializes the progress write and the results-file append so concurrent
# children (introduced by the parallel-execution commit) cannot interleave
# their TTY writes or corrupt the tally file.
run_one() {
  local mode="$1" name="$2" cmd="$3" idx="$4" total="$5"
  local log="$LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log"
  local start end elapsed status rc
  start=$(date +%s)
  set +e
  bash -c "$cmd" >"$log" 2>&1
  rc=$?
  set -e
  end=$(date +%s)
  elapsed=$((end - start))
  case $rc in
    0)  status=PASS ;;
    77) status=SKIP ;;
    *)  status=FAIL ;;
  esac
  (
    flock 9
    case "$status" in
      PASS) ok   "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (${elapsed}s)" ;;
      SKIP) warn "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=77, ${elapsed}s)" ;;
      FAIL) fail "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=$rc, ${elapsed}s) -> $log" ;;
    esac
    printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%d\t%d\n' \
      "$mode" "$name" "$status" "$rc" "$elapsed" >> "$RESULTS_FILE"
  ) 9>"$LOCK_FILE"
}

# Build the workplan: one entry per (mode, name, cmd) tuple. Order
# matters: csr-mode tests first, then pc-mode, then negatives (matching
# the original sequential layout so logs are streamed in the same order).
declare -a WORKPLAN=()
for tm in csr pc; do
  [[ "$MODE" != "both" && "$MODE" != "$tm" ]] && continue
  for entry in "${RT_TESTS[@]}"; do
    rt_name="${entry%%|*}"
    env_prefix="${entry#*|}"
    [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$rt_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
    cmd="TRIGGER_MODE=$tm $env_prefix bash $HARNESS/e2e.sh"
    WORKPLAN+=("$tm|$rt_name|$cmd")
  done
done
for entry in "${NEG_TESTS[@]}"; do
  neg_name="${entry%%|*}"
  neg_cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$neg_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  WORKPLAN+=("neg|$neg_name|$neg_cmd")
done
for entry in "${DEMO_TESTS[@]}"; do
  demo_name="${entry%%|*}"
  demo_cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$demo_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  WORKPLAN+=("demo|$demo_name|$demo_cmd")
done

TOTAL=${#WORKPLAN[@]}
step "logs: $LOG_DIR  jobs: $JOBS  mode: $MODE  tests: $TOTAL"

# FIFO-based bounded job pool. Each child reads one token from $SEM_FIFO
# before starting and writes one back on exit; only $JOBS tokens exist,
# so at most $JOBS children run concurrently. Opening the fifo in
# read-write mode (<>) means writes never block the parent. The fifo
# inode is unlinked immediately after fd 8 is bound -- the kernel keeps
# the pipe alive as long as fd 8 is open.
SEM_FIFO="$LOG_DIR/sem.fifo"
mkfifo "$SEM_FIFO"
exec 8<>"$SEM_FIFO"
rm -f "$SEM_FIFO"
for ((sem_i=0; sem_i<JOBS; sem_i++)); do
  printf '.\n' >&8
done

i=0
for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
  i=$((i+1))
  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
  w_rest="${w#*|}"
  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
  w_cmd="${w_rest#*|}"
  read -r -u 8 _tok
  (
    run_one "$w_mode" "$w_name" "$w_cmd" "$i" "$TOTAL"
    printf '.\n' >&8
  ) &
done
wait
exec 8>&-

declare -A MODE_PASS=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0 [demo]=0)
declare -A MODE_FAIL=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0 [demo]=0)
declare -A MODE_SKIP=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0 [demo]=0)
declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()
while IFS=$'\t' read -r r_mode r_name r_status _r_rc _r_el; do
  case "$r_status" in
    PASS) MODE_PASS[$r_mode]=$((MODE_PASS[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
    SKIP) MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]=$((MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
    FAIL) MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]=$((MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]+1));
          FAILED_NAMES+=("${r_name}[${r_mode}]") ;;
  esac
done < "$RESULTS_FILE"

# Stream failed-test logs in workplan order so the user sees a
# deterministic post-run failure dump.
for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
  w_rest="${w#*|}"
  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
  w_status=$(awk -F'\t' -v m="$w_mode" -v n="$w_name" \
                 '$1==m&&$2==n{print $3; exit}' "$RESULTS_FILE")
  if [[ "$w_status" == "FAIL" ]]; then
    echo
    echo "=== FAIL log: ${w_name}[${w_mode}] ==="
    cat "$LOG_DIR/$w_mode--$w_name.log"
  fi
done

echo
csr_p=${MODE_PASS[csr]} csr_f=${MODE_FAIL[csr]} csr_s=${MODE_SKIP[csr]}
pc_p=${MODE_PASS[pc]}   pc_f=${MODE_FAIL[pc]}   pc_s=${MODE_SKIP[pc]}
neg_p=${MODE_PASS[neg]} neg_f=${MODE_FAIL[neg]} neg_s=${MODE_SKIP[neg]}
dem_p=${MODE_PASS[demo]} dem_f=${MODE_FAIL[demo]} dem_s=${MODE_SKIP[demo]}
total_pass=$((csr_p + pc_p + neg_p + dem_p))
total_fail=$((csr_f + pc_f + neg_f + dem_f))
total_skip=$((csr_s + pc_s + neg_s + dem_s))
step "summary: csr ${csr_p}p/${csr_f}f/${csr_s}s  pc ${pc_p}p/${pc_f}f/${pc_s}s  neg ${neg_p}p/${neg_f}f/${neg_s}s  demo ${dem_p}p/${dem_f}f/${dem_s}s  total ${total_pass} passed, ${total_fail} failed, ${total_skip} skipped"
if (( total_fail > 0 )); then
  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
  exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# RED test for F4: `./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both] [--jobs N]`.
#
# Wraps scripts/run_e2e.sh with JOBS=8 default; parses results.tsv and
# prints a `PASS X, FAIL Y, SKIP Z` summary. Parameter resolution is
# verified via `--dry-run` (same pattern as F2's build subcommand).
# The real smoke step at the end is gated on built artifacts.

set -uo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
HSIM="$ROOT/hsim"

fail() { echo "[FAIL] $*" >&2; exit 1; }
ok()   { echo "[ok] $*"; }

[ -x "$HSIM" ] || fail "missing executable: $HSIM"

# 1. --help mentions --filter, --mode, --jobs.
out=$("$HSIM" test --help 2>&1) || fail "test --help non-zero: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -q -- '--filter' || fail "help missing --filter:"$'\n'"$out"
echo "$out" | grep -q -- '--mode'   || fail "help missing --mode:"$'\n'"$out"
echo "$out" | grep -q -- '--jobs'   || fail "help missing --jobs:"$'\n'"$out"
ok "test --help lists --filter / --mode / --jobs"

# 2. --mode bogus rejected by argparse choices.
set +e
out=$("$HSIM" test --mode bogus --dry-run 2>&1)
rc=$?
set -e
[ "$rc" -ne 0 ] || fail "test --mode bogus should exit non-zero; output: $out"
ok "test --mode bogus exits non-zero (rc=$rc)"

# 3. --dry-run defaults: JOBS=8, MODE=both, FILTER empty.
out=$("$HSIM" test --dry-run 2>&1) || fail "test --dry-run non-zero: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -q '^JOBS=8$'    || fail "default JOBS not 8:"$'\n'"$out"
echo "$out" | grep -q '^MODE=both$' || fail "default MODE not both:"$'\n'"$out"
echo "$out" | grep -q '^FILTER=$'   || fail "default FILTER not empty:"$'\n'"$out"
echo "$out" | grep -q 'bash .*scripts/run_e2e\.sh' || fail "missing exec line:"$'\n'"$out"
ok "test --dry-run defaults JOBS=8 MODE=both FILTER="

# 4. --jobs 4 overrides the cap.
out=$("$HSIM" test --jobs 4 --dry-run 2>&1) || fail "test --jobs 4 non-zero: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -q '^JOBS=4$' || fail "JOBS not 4 with --jobs 4:"$'\n'"$out"
ok "test --jobs 4 overrides default"

# 5. --filter / --mode propagate.
out=$("$HSIM" test --filter '^rt_c_hello$' --mode csr --dry-run 2>&1) \
  || fail "test --filter --mode non-zero: $out"
echo "$out" | grep -q '^FILTER=\^rt_c_hello\$$' || fail "FILTER not propagated:"$'\n'"$out"
echo "$out" | grep -q '^MODE=csr$'              || fail "MODE not propagated:"$'\n'"$out"
ok "test --filter ^rt_c_hello\$ --mode csr propagates env"

# 6. scripts/run_e2e.sh must be unchanged on disk.
diff -q <(git -C "$ROOT" show HEAD:scripts/run_e2e.sh) "$ROOT/scripts/run_e2e.sh" >/dev/null \
  || fail "scripts/run_e2e.sh differs from HEAD"
ok "scripts/run_e2e.sh unchanged from HEAD"

# 7. Real smoke: filtered tiny run -> PASS 1, FAIL 0, SKIP 0.
#    Skipped if the default vsim binary is not present.
VSIM_DEFAULT="$ROOT/verilator/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium"
if [ ! -x "$VSIM_DEFAULT" ]; then
  echo "[skip] real smoke (missing $VSIM_DEFAULT)"
else
  set +e
  out=$(timeout 120 "$HSIM" test --jobs 1 --filter '^rt_c_hello$' --mode csr 2>&1)
  rc=$?
  set -e
  [ "$rc" -eq 0 ] || fail "real smoke rc=$rc:"$'\n'"$out"
  echo "$out" | grep -q '^PASS 1, FAIL 0, SKIP 0' \
    || fail "missing summary line:"$'\n'"$out"
  ok "real smoke: PASS 1, FAIL 0, SKIP 0"
fi

echo "PASS test_hsim_test_small"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""hsim - end-user entry point for the Andes hybrid simulator.

Single-file driver. Wraps the developer-grade scripts (setup.sh,
tests/e2e.sh, scripts/run_e2e.sh) behind four subcommands:

    ./hsim build [--what TARGET]
    ./hsim run <elf-path> --mode csr|pc [--verbose]
    ./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both]
    ./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>

Python 3 stdlib only; no pip deps.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import os
import re
import shlex
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent


# --- F1: list ---------------------------------------------------------------

# M1..M8 short table. Authoritative source: docs/USER_GUIDE.md sections 3-4.
# Kept here so `./hsim list modes` does not need to grep USER_GUIDE at runtime.
MODES = [
    ("M1", "csr",           "csrwi 0x7C0, {0,1} insn match (default)"),
    ("M2", "pc",            "ELF symbols _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc"),
    ("M3", "icount",        "retired-insn count trigger"),
    ("M4", "bbv",           "basic-block vector profiling"),
    ("M5", "slice",         "slice-consume; vsim resumes from a captured slice"),
    ("M6", "archive",       "checkpoint archive replay"),
    ("M7", "replay",        "deterministic re-execution of a captured run"),
    ("M8", "end-of-program", "natural program exit terminates the round-trip"),
]


def parse_fixtures(makefile: Path) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
    """Extract fixture names + family from tests/fixtures/Makefile.

    Returns rows (name, family) in stable, family-grouped order. Family is
    the variable they came from (RT_FIXTURES, RT_C_FIXTURES, ...).
    """
    text = makefile.read_text()
    rows: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
    # Variable definitions look like:
    #   RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases \
    #                  rt_long_roi
    # We capture everything up to a non-continuation newline.
    var_pattern = re.compile(
        r"^(?P<var>[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*)\s*:?=\s*(?P<body>(?:[^\n]*\\\n)*[^\n]*)$",
        re.MULTILINE,
    )
    interesting = {
        "RT_FIXTURES":      "asm",
        "RT_C_FIXTURES":    "c",
        "RT_C_V_FIXTURES":  "c+rvv",
        "PROBE_V_FIXTURES": "probe",
    }
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for m in var_pattern.finditer(text):
        var = m.group("var")
        if var not in interesting:
            continue
        family = interesting[var]
        body = m.group("body").replace("\\\n", " ")
        for name in body.split():
            if name and name not in seen:
                rows.append((name, family))
                seen.add(name)
    # Pick up the legacy hand-listed ELF targets.
    for name in ("handoff_roundtrip",):
        if name not in seen:
            rows.append((name, "asm"))
            seen.add(name)
    return rows


def list_fixtures() -> int:
    makefile = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "fixtures" / "Makefile"
    if not makefile.is_file():
        print(f"hsim: missing {makefile}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    rows = parse_fixtures(makefile)
    width = max(len(name) for name, _ in rows)
    print(f"{'FIXTURE'.ljust(width)}  FAMILY")
    for name, family in rows:
        print(f"{name.ljust(width)}  {family}")
    return 0


def list_demos() -> int:
    demo_dir = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "demos"
    if not demo_dir.is_dir():
        print(f"hsim: missing {demo_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    names = sorted(p.stem for p in demo_dir.glob("demo_*.sh"))
    if not names:
        print(f"hsim: no demos found under {demo_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    for name in names:
        print(name)
    return 0


def list_modes() -> int:
    width_id = max(len(m[0]) for m in MODES)
    width_key = max(len(m[1]) for m in MODES)
    print(f"{'ID'.ljust(width_id)}  {'KEY'.ljust(width_key)}  DESCRIPTION")
    for mode_id, key, desc in MODES:
        print(f"{mode_id.ljust(width_id)}  {key.ljust(width_key)}  {desc}")
    return 0


def cmd_list(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    return {
        "fixtures": list_fixtures,
        "demos":    list_demos,
        "modes":    list_modes,
    }[args.target]()


# --- F2: build --------------------------------------------------------------

# Each target maps to a list of subprocess argv lists. We delegate to the
# existing scripts (setup.sh, scripts/build_*.sh, Makefiles) -- no logic
# duplicated here.
def _build_steps(target: str) -> list[list[str]]:
    qemu     = ["bash", "scripts/build_qemu.sh"]
    vsim     = ["bash", "scripts/build_vsim.sh"]
    plugin   = ["make", "-C", "qemu_plugin", "QEMU_ROOT=./QEMU"]
    simpoint = ["bash", "scripts/build_simpoint.sh"]
    fixtures = ["make", "-C", "tests/fixtures"]
    setup    = ["bash", "setup.sh"]
    return {
        "all":      [setup, fixtures],
        "qemu":     [qemu],
        "vsim":     [vsim],
        "plugin":   [plugin],
        "simpoint": [simpoint],
        "fixtures": [fixtures],
    }[target]


def cmd_build(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    try:
        steps = _build_steps(args.what)
    except KeyError:
        print(f"hsim: unknown build target: {args.what}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    for argv in steps:
        line = shlex.join(argv)
        if args.dry_run:
            print(line)
            continue
        print(f"[hsim] {line}")
        rc = subprocess.run(argv, cwd=REPO_ROOT).returncode
        if rc != 0:
            print(f"hsim: build step failed (rc={rc}): {line}", file=sys.stderr)
            return rc
    return 0


# --- F3: run ----------------------------------------------------------------

# Default build-artifact locations. Honored as env-var overrides so the
# F3 RED test can simulate a missing vsim binary without disturbing the
# real one.
_DEFAULT_ARTIFACTS = {
    "vsim":   REPO_ROOT / "verilator" / "build" / "sim_ax45mpv_premium",
    "qemu":   REPO_ROOT / "build" / "qemu" / "qemu-system-riscv64",
    "plugin": REPO_ROOT / "qemu_plugin" / "libhybrid_handoff.so",
}
_ARTIFACT_ENV = {"vsim": "VSIM_BIN", "qemu": "VSIM_QEMU", "plugin": "VSIM_PLUGIN"}


def _resolve_artifacts() -> dict[str, Path]:
    return {
        label: Path(os.environ.get(_ARTIFACT_ENV[label]) or default)
        for label, default in _DEFAULT_ARTIFACTS.items()
    }


def cmd_run(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    elf = Path(args.elf)
    if not elf.is_absolute():
        elf = (Path.cwd() / elf).resolve()
    if not elf.is_file():
        print(f"hsim: ELF not found: {args.elf}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    artifacts = _resolve_artifacts()
    for label, path in artifacts.items():
        if not path.is_file():
            print(f"hsim: {label} not built -- run ./hsim build", file=sys.stderr)
            return 2

    env = os.environ.copy()
    env["FIXTURE"]      = elf.stem
    env["TRIGGER_MODE"] = args.mode
    env["VSIM_ELF"]     = str(elf)
    env["VSIM_BIN"]     = str(artifacts["vsim"])
    env["VSIM_QEMU"]    = str(artifacts["qemu"])
    env["VSIM_PLUGIN"]  = str(artifacts["plugin"])

    cmd = ["bash", str(REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "e2e.sh")]
    if args.verbose:
        print(f"[hsim] FIXTURE={env['FIXTURE']} TRIGGER_MODE={env['TRIGGER_MODE']} "
              f"VSIM_ELF={env['VSIM_ELF']}", flush=True)
        print(f"[hsim] exec: {shlex.join(cmd)}", flush=True)
    return subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=REPO_ROOT, env=env).returncode


# --- main -------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="hsim",
        description="End-user driver for the Andes hybrid simulator (QEMU + vsim).",
    )
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", metavar="<cmd>")
    sub.required = True

    p_list = sub.add_parser(
        "list",
        help="list available fixtures, demos, or trigger modes",
    )
    p_list.add_argument(
        "target",
        choices=("fixtures", "demos", "modes"),
        help="what to list",
    )
    p_list.set_defaults(func=cmd_list)

    p_build = sub.add_parser(
        "build",
        help="build artifacts (qemu, vsim, plugin, simpoint, ELF fixtures)",
    )
    p_build.add_argument(
        "--what",
        choices=("all", "qemu", "vsim", "plugin", "simpoint", "fixtures"),
        default="all",
        help="which target to build (default: all)",
    )
    p_build.add_argument(
        "--dry-run",
        action="store_true",
        help="print the underlying commands without executing",
    )
    p_build.set_defaults(func=cmd_build)

    p_run = sub.add_parser(
        "run",
        help="run one ELF through the QEMU -> vsim -> QEMU round-trip",
    )
    p_run.add_argument(
        "elf",
        help="path to a RISC-V ELF (relative or absolute)",
    )
    p_run.add_argument(
        "--mode",
        choices=("csr", "pc"),
        required=True,
        help="trigger mode: csr (csrwi 0x7C0,{0,1}) or pc (ELF symbols)",
    )
    p_run.add_argument(
        "-v", "--verbose",
        action="store_true",
        help="print resolved env and command before launching",
    )
    p_run.set_defaults(func=cmd_run)

    return parser


def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    return args.func(args)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main(sys.argv[1:]))

# --- F4: test ---------------------------------------------------------------

# Default JOBS cap. The 128-core dev host saturates if scripts/run_e2e.sh's
# default (~nproc) is used, so we pin to 8. --jobs N overrides.
_DEFAULT_JOBS = 8


def _summarize_results(log_dir: Path) -> tuple[int, int, int, list[Path]]:
    results = log_dir / "results.tsv"
    p = f = s = 0
    failed_logs: list[Path] = []
    if not results.is_file():
        return p, f, s, failed_logs
    for line in results.read_text().splitlines():
        parts = line.split("\t")
        if len(parts) < 3:
            continue
        mode, name, status = parts[0], parts[1], parts[2]
        if status == "PASS":
            p += 1
        elif status == "FAIL":
            f += 1
            failed_logs.append(log_dir / f"{mode}--{name}.log")
        elif status == "SKIP":
            s += 1
    return p, f, s, failed_logs


def cmd_test(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    artifacts = _resolve_artifacts()
    log_dir = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="hsim-test-"))

    env = os.environ.copy()
    env["JOBS"]    = str(args.jobs)
    env["MODE"]    = args.mode
    env["LOG_DIR"] = str(log_dir)
    env["FILTER"]  = args.filter or ""
    env["VSIM_BIN"]    = str(artifacts["vsim"])
    env["VSIM_QEMU"]   = str(artifacts["qemu"])
    env["VSIM_PLUGIN"] = str(artifacts["plugin"])

    cmd = ["bash", str(REPO_ROOT / "scripts" / "run_e2e.sh")]
    if args.dry_run:
        print(f"JOBS={env['JOBS']}")
        print(f"MODE={env['MODE']}")
        print(f"FILTER={env['FILTER']}")
        print(f"LOG_DIR={env['LOG_DIR']}")
        print(f"exec: {shlex.join(cmd)}")
        return 0

    start = time.monotonic()
    rc = subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=REPO_ROOT, env=env).returncode
    elapsed = time.monotonic() - start

    p, f, s, failed_logs = _summarize_results(log_dir)
    print(f"PASS {p}, FAIL {f}, SKIP {s} (elapsed: {elapsed:.1f}s)")
    if failed_logs:
        print("Failed test logs:")
        for fp in failed_logs:
            print(f"  {fp}")
    return rc


# --- main -------------------------------------------------------------------

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""hsim - end-user entry point for the Andes hybrid simulator.

Single-file driver. Wraps the developer-grade scripts (setup.sh,
tests/e2e.sh, scripts/run_e2e.sh) behind four subcommands:

    ./hsim build [--what TARGET]
    ./hsim run <elf-path> --mode csr|pc [--verbose]
    ./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both]
    ./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>

Python 3 stdlib only; no pip deps.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import os
import re
import shlex
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
import time
from pathlib import Path


REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent


# --- F1: list ---------------------------------------------------------------

# M1..M8 short table. Authoritative source: docs/USER_GUIDE.md sections 3-4.
# Kept here so `./hsim list modes` does not need to grep USER_GUIDE at runtime.
MODES = [
    ("M1", "csr",           "csrwi 0x7C0, {0,1} insn match (default)"),
    ("M2", "pc",            "ELF symbols _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc"),
    ("M3", "icount",        "retired-insn count trigger"),
    ("M4", "bbv",           "basic-block vector profiling"),
    ("M5", "slice",         "slice-consume; vsim resumes from a captured slice"),
    ("M6", "archive",       "checkpoint archive replay"),
    ("M7", "replay",        "deterministic re-execution of a captured run"),
    ("M8", "end-of-program", "natural program exit terminates the round-trip"),
]


def parse_fixtures(makefile: Path) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
    """Extract fixture names + family from tests/fixtures/Makefile.

    Returns rows (name, family) in stable, family-grouped order. Family is
    the variable they came from (RT_FIXTURES, RT_C_FIXTURES, ...).
    """
    text = makefile.read_text()
    rows: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
    # Variable definitions look like:
    #   RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases \
    #                  rt_long_roi
    # We capture everything up to a non-continuation newline.
    var_pattern = re.compile(
        r"^(?P<var>[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*)\s*:?=\s*(?P<body>(?:[^\n]*\\\n)*[^\n]*)$",
        re.MULTILINE,
    )
    interesting = {
        "RT_FIXTURES":      "asm",
        "RT_C_FIXTURES":    "c",
        "RT_C_V_FIXTURES":  "c+rvv",
        "PROBE_V_FIXTURES": "probe",
    }
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for m in var_pattern.finditer(text):
        var = m.group("var")
        if var not in interesting:
            continue
        family = interesting[var]
        body = m.group("body").replace("\\\n", " ")
        for name in body.split():
            if name and name not in seen:
                rows.append((name, family))
                seen.add(name)
    # Pick up the legacy hand-listed ELF targets.
    for name in ("handoff_roundtrip",):
        if name not in seen:
            rows.append((name, "asm"))
            seen.add(name)
    return rows


def list_fixtures() -> int:
    makefile = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "fixtures" / "Makefile"
    if not makefile.is_file():
        print(f"hsim: missing {makefile}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    rows = parse_fixtures(makefile)
    width = max(len(name) for name, _ in rows)
    print(f"{'FIXTURE'.ljust(width)}  FAMILY")
    for name, family in rows:
        print(f"{name.ljust(width)}  {family}")
    return 0


def list_demos() -> int:
    demo_dir = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "demos"
    if not demo_dir.is_dir():
        print(f"hsim: missing {demo_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    names = sorted(p.stem for p in demo_dir.glob("demo_*.sh"))
    if not names:
        print(f"hsim: no demos found under {demo_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    for name in names:
        print(name)
    return 0


def list_modes() -> int:
    width_id = max(len(m[0]) for m in MODES)
    width_key = max(len(m[1]) for m in MODES)
    print(f"{'ID'.ljust(width_id)}  {'KEY'.ljust(width_key)}  DESCRIPTION")
    for mode_id, key, desc in MODES:
        print(f"{mode_id.ljust(width_id)}  {key.ljust(width_key)}  {desc}")
    return 0


def cmd_list(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    return {
        "fixtures": list_fixtures,
        "demos":    list_demos,
        "modes":    list_modes,
    }[args.target]()


# --- F2: build --------------------------------------------------------------

# Each target maps to a list of subprocess argv lists. We delegate to the
# existing scripts (setup.sh, scripts/build_*.sh, Makefiles) -- no logic
# duplicated here.
def _build_steps(target: str) -> list[list[str]]:
    qemu     = ["bash", "scripts/build_qemu.sh"]
    vsim     = ["bash", "scripts/build_vsim.sh"]
    plugin   = ["make", "-C", "qemu_plugin", "QEMU_ROOT=./QEMU"]
    simpoint = ["bash", "scripts/build_simpoint.sh"]
    fixtures = ["make", "-C", "tests/fixtures"]
    setup    = ["bash", "setup.sh"]
    return {
        "all":      [setup, fixtures],
        "qemu":     [qemu],
        "vsim":     [vsim],
        "plugin":   [plugin],
        "simpoint": [simpoint],
        "fixtures": [fixtures],
    }[target]


def cmd_build(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    try:
        steps = _build_steps(args.what)
    except KeyError:
        print(f"hsim: unknown build target: {args.what}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2
    for argv in steps:
        line = shlex.join(argv)
        if args.dry_run:
            print(line)
            continue
        print(f"[hsim] {line}")
        rc = subprocess.run(argv, cwd=REPO_ROOT).returncode
        if rc != 0:
            print(f"hsim: build step failed (rc={rc}): {line}", file=sys.stderr)
            return rc
    return 0


# --- F3: run ----------------------------------------------------------------

# Default build-artifact locations. Honored as env-var overrides so the
# F3 RED test can simulate a missing vsim binary without disturbing the
# real one.
_DEFAULT_ARTIFACTS = {
    "vsim":   REPO_ROOT / "verilator" / "build" / "sim_ax45mpv_premium",
    "qemu":   REPO_ROOT / "build" / "qemu" / "qemu-system-riscv64",
    "plugin": REPO_ROOT / "qemu_plugin" / "libhybrid_handoff.so",
}
_ARTIFACT_ENV = {"vsim": "VSIM_BIN", "qemu": "VSIM_QEMU", "plugin": "VSIM_PLUGIN"}


def _resolve_artifacts() -> dict[str, Path]:
    return {
        label: Path(os.environ.get(_ARTIFACT_ENV[label]) or default)
        for label, default in _DEFAULT_ARTIFACTS.items()
    }


def cmd_run(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    elf = Path(args.elf)
    if not elf.is_absolute():
        elf = (Path.cwd() / elf).resolve()
    if not elf.is_file():
        print(f"hsim: ELF not found: {args.elf}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    artifacts = _resolve_artifacts()
    for label, path in artifacts.items():
        if not path.is_file():
            print(f"hsim: {label} not built -- run ./hsim build", file=sys.stderr)
            return 2

    env = os.environ.copy()
    env["FIXTURE"]      = elf.stem
    env["TRIGGER_MODE"] = args.mode
    env["VSIM_ELF"]     = str(elf)
    env["VSIM_BIN"]     = str(artifacts["vsim"])
    env["VSIM_QEMU"]    = str(artifacts["qemu"])
    env["VSIM_PLUGIN"]  = str(artifacts["plugin"])

    cmd = ["bash", str(REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "e2e.sh")]
    if args.verbose:
        print(f"[hsim] FIXTURE={env['FIXTURE']} TRIGGER_MODE={env['TRIGGER_MODE']} "
              f"VSIM_ELF={env['VSIM_ELF']}", flush=True)
        print(f"[hsim] exec: {shlex.join(cmd)}", flush=True)
    return subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=REPO_ROOT, env=env).returncode


# --- main -------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="hsim",
        description="End-user driver for the Andes hybrid simulator (QEMU + vsim).",
    )
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", metavar="<cmd>")
    sub.required = True

    p_list = sub.add_parser(
        "list",
        help="list available fixtures, demos, or trigger modes",
    )
    p_list.add_argument(
        "target",
        choices=("fixtures", "demos", "modes"),
        help="what to list",
    )
    p_list.set_defaults(func=cmd_list)

    p_build = sub.add_parser(
        "build",
        help="build artifacts (qemu, vsim, plugin, simpoint, ELF fixtures)",
    )
    p_build.add_argument(
        "--what",
        choices=("all", "qemu", "vsim", "plugin", "simpoint", "fixtures"),
        default="all",
        help="which target to build (default: all)",
    )
    p_build.add_argument(
        "--dry-run",
        action="store_true",
        help="print the underlying commands without executing",
    )
    p_build.set_defaults(func=cmd_build)

    p_run = sub.add_parser(
        "run",
        help="run one ELF through the QEMU -> vsim -> QEMU round-trip",
    )
    p_run.add_argument(
        "elf",
        help="path to a RISC-V ELF (relative or absolute)",
    )
    p_run.add_argument(
        "--mode",
        choices=("csr", "pc"),
        required=True,
        help="trigger mode: csr (csrwi 0x7C0,{0,1}) or pc (ELF symbols)",
    )
    p_run.add_argument(
        "-v", "--verbose",
        action="store_true",
        help="print resolved env and command before launching",
    )
    p_run.set_defaults(func=cmd_run)

    return parser


def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    return args.func(args)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main(sys.argv[1:]))

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# ROADMAP: `./hsim` end-user driver

Tracks the rollout of a single, end-user-friendly entry point
(`./hsim`) that hides the developer-grade plumbing currently exposed
by `setup.sh`, `tests/e2e.sh`, and `scripts/run_e2e.sh`.

Plan source-of-truth: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/i-need-to-design-purring-kahan.md`.

One session = one feature. Update status when starting (in-progress)
and on completion (done). Each feature is its own commit (or a small
structural-then-behavioral pair), never mixed.

## Status Legend

- not-started: feature has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: RED test was written, GREEN landed, and the feature's done-criteria pass

## Global Invariant

After every feature lands, this must hold:

```
JOBS=8 MODE=both bash scripts/run_e2e.sh
# pass list == 45 PASS / 0 FAIL  (baseline from e2e_ROADMAP.md F0)
```

`./hsim` only WRAPS the existing scripts; it MUST NOT change their
behavior. Any divergence from the e2e baseline blocks the next feature.

## Dependency DAG

```
F1 (list) --> F2 (build) --> F3 (run) --> F4 (test) --> F5 (docs) --> F6 (verify)
                                                            |
                                                            +-- can also land any time after F4
```

Strict serial order F1 -> F4 because each later feature reuses the
`Paths` / `run_subprocess` helpers introduced by the earlier ones.
F5 (docs) may land between F4 and F6 or after F6.

## Pre-flight decisions (locked at plan time)

1. **Entry point name** -- `./hsim` at repo root (executable Python script).
2. **Language** -- Python 3, stdlib only (no pip deps).
3. **Run scope** -- accepts both fixture-by-name and arbitrary ELF paths.
4. **Missing artifacts** -- fail with one-line hint, no auto-build.
5. **Trigger mode** -- explicit `--mode csr|pc` required in v1.
6. **Output style** -- quiet by default, `--verbose` flag exists.
7. **Out of scope for v1** -- `demo`, `doctor`, auto-detect mode, M3..M8.

## Features

### F1 - `./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>`

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral (new code)
- Description: Lowest-risk subcommand -- pure parsing, no subprocess.
  Lets the user discover fixture names without grepping the Makefile.
- Key files:
  - **Create:** `hsim` (Python 3 script, ~80 lines for this feature only).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_list.sh` (RED test).
- Dependencies: none.
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim list fixtures` exits 0 and outputs `rt_c_hello` and `rt_all_gprs`.
  - `./hsim list demos` exits 0 and outputs `demo_csr_oneshot` and `demo_pc_oneshot`.
  - `./hsim list modes` exits 0 and outputs `M1` and `M8`.
  - `./hsim list bogus` exits 2 with a usage hint.
  - The RED test in `tests/hsim/test_hsim_list.sh` is green.

### F2 - `./hsim build [--what TARGET]`

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: One entry point for all artifact builds. Wraps
  `setup.sh` (qemu, vsim, plugin, simpoint) and
  `make -C tests/fixtures` (ELF fixtures). No reimplementation -- the
  subprocess delegates to the existing scripts.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `build` subparser).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_build_dry.sh` (RED test with `--dry-run`).
- Dependencies: F1 (shares argparse skeleton + `Paths` helper).
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim build --dry-run` prints the underlying `bash setup.sh` /
    `make -C tests/fixtures` commands without executing.
  - `./hsim build --what qemu --dry-run` prints only the QEMU build line.
  - `./hsim build` from a clean checkout produces all four artifacts +
    fixtures and exits 0.
  - `setup.sh` is unchanged on disk.

### F3 - `./hsim run <elf-path> --mode csr|pc`

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: One-shot round-trip runner. Wraps `tests/e2e.sh` with
  the right env vars. Path-only contract: the positional arg is always
  an ELF path (relative resolves against CWD, absolute is honored).
  No bare-fixture-name shortcut. Quiet 3-line progress output by
  default; `--verbose` prefixes the resolved env + command line.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `run` subparser + path-resolution helper +
    artifact pre-flight).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_run_smoke.sh` (RED test).
- Dependencies: F1 (Paths helper), F2 (so artifacts can be built first).
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf` (no `--mode`) exits 2
    with usage hint mentioning `--mode`.
  - `./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf --mode csr` on a clean
    build exits 0 in <30s.
  - `./hsim run /abs/path/foo.elf --mode pc` resolves the path and runs.
  - `VSIM_BIN=/no/such/vsim ./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf --mode csr`
    exits 2 with `hsim: vsim not built -- run ./hsim build`.
  - `--verbose` prints the resolved FIXTURE/TRIGGER_MODE/VSIM_ELF and
    the `bash tests/e2e.sh` command before launching it.

### F4 - `./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both] [--jobs N]`

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Wraps `scripts/run_e2e.sh` with the JOBS=8 cap pre-set
  (per project memory for the 128-core host). Parses the script's
  `results.tsv` and prints a summary line `PASS X, FAIL Y, SKIP Z`.
  On failure, prints the per-test log paths.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `hsim` (add `test` subparser, results.tsv parser).
  - **Create:** `tests/hsim/test_hsim_test_small.sh` (RED test that
    runs `./hsim test --jobs 1 --filter rt_c_hello --mode csr`).
- Dependencies: F1, F2, F3.
- Done criteria:
  - `./hsim test --jobs 1 --filter rt_c_hello --mode csr` exits 0 and
    prints `PASS 1, FAIL 0, SKIP 0` (plus elapsed time).
  - `./hsim test` (no args) caps at JOBS=8 by default.
  - `./hsim test --jobs 4` overrides the cap.
  - `scripts/run_e2e.sh` is unchanged on disk.

### F5 - Docs: README + USER_GUIDE + CLAUDE.md

- Status: not-started
- Type: structural (no code change; doc edits)
- Description: Make `./hsim` the canonical entry point in every
  user-visible doc. The dev-facing env-var surface stays documented
  but is demoted to "escape hatch" sections.
- Key files:
  - **Modify:** `README.md` -- replace the stale `./build.sh`
    quickstart block (lines 12-22) with `./hsim` quickstart.
  - **Modify:** `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` -- prepend a new
    "Section 0.5: Quick start with `./hsim`" before the existing
    Section 1 build instructions. Keep Sections 1-7 unchanged.
  - **Modify:** `CLAUDE.md` -- under "Build Commands" add `./hsim build`
    and `./hsim test` as the primary path; mark raw scripts as
    developer escape hatch.
- Dependencies: F1..F4 (so the examples in docs work).
- Done criteria:
  - README quickstart is 3 lines and uses only `./hsim` commands.
  - USER_GUIDE Section 0.5 has a copy-pastable 4-command worked
    example.
  - CLAUDE.md still lists the underlying scripts for developers but
    points to `./hsim` first.

### F6 - End-to-end verification

- Status: not-started
- Type: no commit (safety net)
- Description: Run the 11-step smoke sequence from the plan against a
  clean checkout. Tick each step.
- Steps:
  ```
  ./hsim                                                          # help
  ./hsim list fixtures                                            # ~17 fixtures
  ./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf --mode csr             # exit 2 if no build
  ./hsim build                                                    # ~5 min cold
  ./hsim list fixtures                                            # unchanged
  ./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf --mode csr             # PASS ~6s
  ./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_hello.elf --mode csr -v          # verbose
  ./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_all_gprs.elf --mode csr            # arbitrary path
  ./hsim run tests/fixtures/rt_c_v_regs.elf --mode pc             # PC trigger
  ./hsim test --filter rt_c --mode csr                            # ~7 tests
  ./hsim test                                                     # full suite
  ```
- Dependencies: F1..F5.
- Done criteria: all 11 steps exit as expected; the full-suite line
  reports the same 45 PASS / 0 FAIL as the e2e baseline.

## Out of scope (deferred to a future driver_ROADMAP_v2)

- `./hsim demo <T1..T7>` -- wrap `tests/demos/demo_*.sh`.
- `./hsim doctor` -- env-health diagnoser.
- `./hsim run --mode auto` -- auto-detect via ELF inspection.
- Building user's own non-hybrid-compatible ELF (instrument it for them).
- M3..M8 (icount, BBV, slice, archive, replay) -- currently only
  reachable via the demo scripts.
- Bash-completion file.

3	Workspace for the Andes hybrid simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim
4	(SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) sharing memory and architectural state
5	across a magic-CSR boundary.
6

8	
9	- `QEMU/` - QEMU fork hosting the TCG plugin
10	- `verilator/` - vsim sources, including `hybrid/qemu_plugin/` (plugin source)
11	
12	## Quickstart
13	
14	```sh
15	git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
16	cd hybrid_sim
17	./build.sh           # qemu, vsim, plugin (in that order)
18	./build.sh test      # ctest -L hybrid-e2e
19	```
20	
21	Subcommands: `qemu`, `vsim`, `plugin`, `test`, `clean`. Run `./build.sh -h`
22	for the full list.
